title: Systems and methods for improved analysis of electromagnetic spectra
abstract: Systems and methods for characterizing electromagnetic spectra are described. The techniques include identifying, using radiation spectroscopy information obtained over a measurement time period, one or more energies associated with electromagnetic radiation emitted by a material or region. The identification includes dividing the measurement time period into two or more subperiods, identifying, for each of the two or more subperiods, measured radiation energies in a subset of the radiation spectroscopy information associated with one of the two or more subperiods, and selecting, from the identified radiation energies for each of the two or more subperiods, radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods. The techniques further include identifying, using the selected radiation energies, one or more radioisotopes characterized by the identified radiation energies.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: CLAIMS 1. A method of characterizing a composition of a material comprising one or more radioisotopes, the method comprising: identifying, using radiation spectroscopy information obtained over a measurement time period, one or more energies associated with electromagnetic radiation emitted by the material by: dividing the measurement time period into two or more subperiods; identifying, for each of the two or more subperiods, measured radiation energies in a subset of the radiation spectroscopy information associated with one of the two or more subperiods; and selecting, from the identified radiation energies for each of the two or more subperiods, radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods; and identifying, using the selected radiation energies, one or more radioisotopes present in the composition of the material. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising generating, using the identified one or more radioisotopes, a certificate of analysis associated with the material. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, further comprising determining, using the selected radiation energies and the radiation spectroscopy information, at least one of: an atom ratio of the identified one or more radioisotopes, an atom percent of the identified one or more radioisotopes, a weight percent of the identified one or more radioisotopes, and/or a relative atomic weight. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, further comprising determining a half-life of a first radioisotope of the identified one or more radioisotopes by: identifying a first magnitude of a measured radiation energy associated with the first radioisotope in a first subperiod of the two or more subperiods; 

 identifying a second magnitude of a measured radiation energy associated with the first radioisotope in a second subperiod of the two or more subperiods, the second subperiod having been measured at a later time during the measurement time period than the first subperiod; determining the half-life of the first radioisotope using a difference between the first magnitude and the second magnitude and a length of time between the first subperiod and the second subperiod. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, further comprising obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information by measuring the electromagnetic radiation using a detector. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein measuring the electromagnetic radiation using a detector comprises using at least one of a high-purity germanium (HPGe) detector, a sodium iodide (Nal) detector, a silicon lithium (SiLi) detector, and/or a passivated implanted planar silicon (PIPS) detector. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information further comprises converting an analog signal generated by the detector to a digital signal using at least one analog-to-digital converter (ADC). 8. The method of claim 7, wherein converting the analog signal to the digital signal using the at least one ADC comprises using two ADCs arranged in a phase-locked loop. 9. The method of claim 7 or 8, wherein obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information further comprises time stamping peaks in the digital signal, the peaks being associated with detection of electromagnetic radiation by the detector. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein time stamping the peaks in the digital signal comprises time stamping the peaks in the digital signal based on a clock rate of the at least one ADC. 11. The method of claim 9 or 10, wherein the subset of the radiation spectroscopy information is determined using the time-stamped peaks in the digital signal. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the radiation spectroscopy information is further obtained by generating, using a voltage signal obtained using a radiation detector during the measurement time period, a histogram of total counts of detected electromagnetic radiation as a function of associated radiation energies. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein generating the histogram further comprises: determining a bin width value associated with a first maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a first minimum full width at half maximum (FWHM) value of a peak of the histogram; determining a DC offset value associated with a second maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a second minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram; and using the determined bin width value and DC offset value to generate the histogram. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein determining the bin width value and/or the DC offset value comprises using a genetic algorithm. 15. The method of any one of claims 12-14, wherein generating the histogram further comprises generated a filtered signal by filtering the voltage signal obtained using the radiation detector using a first moving average, wherein a sampling window associated with the first moving average is selected by determining a first sampling window value associated with a third maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a third minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising: generating a reshaped voltage signal by applying a trapezoid or Gaussian filter to the filtered signal; and filtering the reshaped voltage signal using a second moving average, wherein a sampling window associated with the second moving average is selected by determining a second sampling window value associated with a fourth maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a fourth minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein selecting the radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods comprises selecting radiation energies not associated with noise. 18. A system comprising: at least one computer hardware processor, and at least one non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing processorexecutable instructions that, when executed by the at least one computer hardware processor, cause the at least one computer hardware processor to perform a method comprising: identifying, using radiation spectroscopy information obtained over a measurement time period, one or more energies associated with electromagnetic radiation emitted by a measured material by: dividing the measurement time period into two or more subperiods; identifying, for each of the two or more subperiods, measured radiation energies in a subset of the radiation spectroscopy information associated with one of the two or more subperiods; and selecting, from the identified radiation energies for each of the two or more subperiods, radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods; and identifying, using the selected radiation energies, one or more radioisotopes present in a composition of the material. 19. The system of claim 18, further comprising generating, using the identified one or more radioisotopes, a certificate of analysis associated with the material. 20. The system of claim 18 or 19, further comprising determining, using the selected radiation energies and the radiation spectroscopy information, at least one of: an atom ratio of the identified one or more radioisotopes, an atom percent of the identified one or more radioisotopes, a weight percent of the identified one or more radioisotopes, and/or a relative atomic weight. 21. The system of any one of claims 18-20, further comprising determining a half-life of a first radioisotope of the identified one or more radioisotopes by: identifying a first magnitude of a measured radiation energy associated with the first radioisotope in a first subperiod of the two or more subperiods; identifying a second magnitude of a measured radiation energy associated with the first radioisotope in a second subperiod of the two or more subperiods, the second subperiod having been measured at a later time during the measurement time period than the first subperiod; determining the half-life of the first radioisotope using a difference between the first magnitude and the second magnitude and a length of time between the first subperiod and the second subperiod. 22. The system of any one of claims 18-21, further comprising a detector, wherein obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information comprises measuring the electromagnetic radiation using the detector. 23. The system of claim 22, wherein the detector comprises at least one of a high-purity germanium (HPGe) detector, a sodium iodide (Nal) detector, a silicon lithium (SiLi) detector, and/or a passivated implanted planar silicon (PIPS) detector. 24. The system of claim 22, further comprising at least one analog-to-digital converter (ADC), wherein obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information comprises converting an analog signal generated by the detector to a digital signal using the at least one ADC. 25. The system of claim 24, wherein the at least one ADC comprises two ADCs arranged in a phase-locked loop. 26. The system of claim 24 or 25, wherein obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information further comprises time stamping peaks in the digital signal, the peaks being associated with detection of electromagnetic radiation by the detector. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein time stamping the peaks in the digital signal comprises time stamping the peaks in the digital signal based on a clock rate of the at least one ADC. 28. The system of claim 26 or 27, wherein the subset of the radiation spectroscopy information is determined using the time-stamped peaks in the digital signal. 29. The system of any one of claims 18-28, wherein the radiation spectroscopy information is further obtained by generating, using a voltage signal obtained using a radiation detector during the measurement time period, a histogram of total counts of detected electromagnetic radiation as a function of associated radiation energies. 30. The system of claim 29, wherein generating the histogram further comprises: determining a bin width value associated with a first maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a first minimum full width at half maximum (FWHM) value of a peak of the histogram; determining a DC offset value associated with a second maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a second minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram; and using the determined bin width value and DC offset value to generate the histogram. 31. The system of claim 29 or 30, wherein generating the histogram further comprises generated a filtered signal by filtering the voltage signal obtained using the radiation detector using a first moving average, wherein a sampling window associated with the first moving average is selected by determining a first sampling window value associated with a third maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a third minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram. 32. The system of claim 31, further comprising: generating a reshaped voltage signal by applying a trapezoid or Gaussian filter to the filtered signal; and filtering the reshaped voltage signal using a second moving average, wherein a sampling window associated with the second moving average is selected by determining a second sampling window value associated with a fourth maximum amplitude of a peak of the histogram and/or a fourth minimum FWHM value of a peak of the histogram. 33. The system of any one of claims 18-32, wherein selecting the radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods comprises selecting radiation energies not associated with noise. 34. At least one non-transitory computer readable storage medium storing processorexecutable instructions that, when executed by at least one computer hardware processor, cause the at least one computer hardware processor to perform a method comprising: identifying, using radiation spectroscopy information obtained over a measurement time period, one or more energies associated with electromagnetic radiation emitted by a measured material by: dividing the measurement time period into two or more subperiods; identifying, for each of the two or more subperiods, measured radiation energies in a subset of the radiation spectroscopy information associated with one of the two or more subperiods; and selecting, from the identified radiation energies for each of the two or more subperiods, radiation energies identified in at least two of the two or more subperiods; and identifying, using the selected radiation energies, one or more radioisotopes present in a composition of the material. 35. The at least one non-transitory computer readable storage medium of any one of claims 34-37, the method further comprising obtaining the radiation spectroscopy information by measuring the electromagnetic radiation using a detector. 39. The at least one non-transitory computer readable storage medium of any one of claims 34-38, wherein the radiation spectroscopy information is further obtained by generating, using a voltage signal obtained using a radiation detector during the measurement time period, a histogram of total counts of detected electromagnetic radiation as a function of associated radiation energies. 40.
